<asp>
% 4‑connected neighbourhood offsets (kept for completeness)
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  Cells that contain a 2 – these will be the “seeds” that colour
%  interior regions.
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
seed2(R,C) :- input(R,C,2).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  Does a 2‑seed have a free neighbour to its left?
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
has_left(Rseed,Cseed) :-
    seed2(Rseed,Cseed),
    Cseed > 0,
    not input(Rseed,Cseed-1,3).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  Does a 2‑seed have a free neighbour below?
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
neighbor_down(Rseed,Cseed,Rd,Cd,V) :-
    delta(DR,DC),
    DR = 1, DC = 0,
    Rd = Rseed+DR,
    Cd = Cseed+DC,
    input(Rd,Cd,V).
has_down(Rseed,Cseed) :-
    seed2(Rseed,Cseed),
    neighbor_down(Rseed,Cseed,Rd,Cd,V),
    V != 3.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  Region coloured 2 (col2) – cells reachable from a 2‑seed
%  without crossing a 3‑wall, either leftward (if free left) or
%  downward (if free down).
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
col2(R,C) :-
    seed2(Rseed,Cseed),
    R = Rseed,
    C <= Cseed,
    has_left(Rseed,Cseed),
    not input(R,C,3).

col2(R,C) :-
    seed2(Rseed,Cseed),
    C = Cseed,
    R >= Rseed,
    has_down(Rseed,Cseed),
    not input(R,C,3).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  Exactly one colour is allowed for each cell.
%  - 3 stays 3 (wall)
%  - 2‑seeds colour their reachable interior cells 2
%  - 0’s outside the 2‑region stay 0
%  - 0’s inside the 2‑region become 2 (already covered by col2)
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
allowed(R,C,Color) :- input(R,C,3),        Color = 3.
allowed(R,C,Color) :- col2(R,C),        input(R,C,0), Color = 2.
allowed(R,C,Color) :- col2(R,C),        input(R,C,2), Color = 2.
allowed(R,C,Color) :- not col2(R,C),    input(R,C,0), Color = 0.
allowed(R,C,Color) :- not col2(R,C),    input(R,C,V), V != 0, Color = V.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  Each cell must produce exactly one output colour.
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
output_cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

% Choose the allowed colour for every output cell – exactly one
% answer set is forced.
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :-
    output_cell(R, C), allowed(R, C, Color).
</asp>

```
% 4‑connected neighbourhood offsets (kept for completeness)
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  Cells that contain a 2 – these will be the “seeds” that colour
%  interior regions.
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
seed2(R,C) :- input(R,C,2).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  Does a 2‑seed have a free neighbour to its left?
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
has_left(Rseed,Cseed) :-
    seed2(Rseed,Cseed),
    Cseed > 0,
    not input(Rseed,Cseed-1,3).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  Does a 2‑seed have a free neighbour below?
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
neighbor_down(Rseed,Cseed,Rd,Cd,V) :-
    delta(DR,DC),
    DR = 1, DC = 0,
    Rd = Rseed+DR,
    Cd = Cseed+DC,
    input(Rd,Cd,V).
has_down(Rseed,Cseed) :-
    seed2(Rseed,Cseed),
    neighbor_down(Rseed,Cseed,Rd,Cd,V),
    V != 3.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  Region coloured 2 (col2) – cells reachable from a 2‑seed
%  without crossing a 3‑wall, either leftward (if free left) or
%  downward (if free down).
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
col2(R,C) :-
    seed2(Rseed,Cseed),
    R = Rseed,
    C <= Cseed,
    has_left(Rseed,Cseed),
    not input(R,C,3).

col2(R,C) :-
    seed2(Rseed,Cseed),
    C = Cseed,
    R >= Rseed,
    has_down(Rseed,Cseed),
    not input(R,C,3).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  Exactly one colour is allowed for each cell.
%  - 3 stays 3 (wall)
%  - 2‑seeds colour their reachable interior cells 2
%  - 0’s outside the 2‑region stay 0
%  - 0’s inside the 2‑region become 2 (already covered by col2)
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
allowed(R,C,Color) :- input(R,C,3),        Color = 3.
allowed(R,C,Color) :- col2(R,C),        input(R,C,0), Color = 2.
allowed(R,C,Color) :- col2(R,C),        input(R,C,2), Color = 2.
allowed(R,C,Color) :- not col2(R,C),    input(R,C,0), Color = 0.
allowed(R,C,Color) :- not col2(R,C),    input(R,C,V), V != 0, Color = V.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  Each cell must produce exactly one output colour.
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
output_cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

% Choose the allowed colour for every output cell – exactly one
% answer set is forced.
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :-
    output_cell(R, C), allowed(R, C, Color).
```

% -------------------------------------------------------------
%  Grid dimensions
% -------------------------------------------------------------
n_rows(N)   :- N = #count{R : input(R,_,_)}.
n_cols(M)   :- M = #count{C : input(_,C,_)}.

% -------------------------------------------------------------
%  4‑connected neighbourhood (no extra delta predicate)
% -------------------------------------------------------------
neighbor(R1,C1,R,C) :-
    input(R1,C1,_),
    input(R,C,_),
    (R = R1+1, C = C1) ;      % up
neighbor(R1,C1,R,C) :-
    input(R1,C1,_),
    input(R,C,_),
    (R = R1-1, C = C1) ;      % down
neighbor(R1,C1,R,C) :-
    input(R1,C1,_),
    input(R,C,_),
    (R = R1,   C = C1+1) ;    % right
neighbor(R1,C1,R,C) :-
    input(R1,C1,_),
    input(R,C,_),
    (R = R1,   C = C1-1).     % left

% -------------------------------------------------------------
%  Border cells – any cell on the outer edge
% -------------------------------------------------------------
border(R,C) :- input(R,C,_), (R = 0 ; R = N-1 ; C = 0 ; C = M-1).

% -------------------------------------------------------------
%  Outside – start from border non‑wall cells and flood‑fill
% -------------------------------------------------------------
outside(R,C) :- border(R,C), not input(R,C,3).
outside(R,C) :-
    outside(R1,C1),
    neighbor(R1,C1,R,C),
    not input(R,C,3).

% -------------------------------------------------------------
%  Interior cells – non‑wall cells that cannot reach the border
% -------------------------------------------------------------
interior(R,C) :- not input(R,C,3), not outside(R,C).

% -------------------------------------------------------------
%  Colour that is allowed for each cell (exactly one colour)
% -------------------------------------------------------------
allowed(R,C,3)          :- input(R,C,3).                       % wall cells stay 3
allowed(R,C,Color)      :- interior(R,C), input(R,C,0),    Color = 2.   % interior zeros become 2
allowed(R,C,Color)      :- interior(R,C), input(R,C,V),    V != 0, V != 3,
                         Color = V.                                      % interior non‑zero keeps colour
allowed(R,C,Color)      :- not interior(R,C), input(R,C,0),   Color = 0.   % outside zeros stay 0
allowed(R,C,Color)      :- not interior(R,C), input(R,C,V),   V != 0,
                         Color = V.                                      % outside non‑zero keeps colour

% -------------------------------------------------------------
%  Every cell must produce exactly one output colour
% -------------------------------------------------------------
output_cell(R,C) :- true.                     % every cell of the output grid

1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :-
    output_cell(R, C), allowed(R, C, Color).

<transformation>
The input is treated as a tile map. The output is NxM times larger than the input (where the input is N rows x M cols). Each input cell at (BR, BC) with a non-zero value causes a copy of the full input to be placed in the corresponding NxM block of the output (at rows BR*N .. BR*N+N-1, cols BC*M .. BC*M+M-1). Each input cell with value 0 produces an all-zero block at that position.
</transformation>

<asp>
% Count distinct row/col indices — equals grid dimensions for 0-indexed inputs
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(N) :- N = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Output is NR*NR rows and NC*NC cols (input tiled by itself)
output_cell(R, C) :-
    input(BR, BC, _), input(DR, DC, _),
    n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
    R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC.

1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Non-zero block: the sub-cell must equal the input value at the relative position
:- input(BR, BC, V), V != 0,
   input(DR, DC, TileColor),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != TileColor.

% Zero block: every sub-cell must be 0
:- input(BR, BC, 0),
   input(DR, DC, _),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != 0.
</asp>

The output is the same size as the input. Color-3 cells are unchanged. Non-3 cells (value 0) that are enclosed by color-3 walls — i.e., cannot reach the grid boundary via 4-connected movement through non-3 cells — are filled with color 4. All other non-3 cells remain 0.

<asp>
% Output has the same dimensions as the input
output_cell(R, C) :- input(R, C, _).
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Grid boundary coordinates
min_row(R) :- R = #min{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
max_row(R) :- R = #max{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
min_col(C) :- C = #min{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.
max_col(C) :- C = #max{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.

% 4-connected adjacency offsets
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% Non-3 cells on the grid boundary are reachable from outside
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_row(Rmin), R = Rmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_row(Rmax), R = Rmax.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_col(Cmin), C = Cmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_col(Cmax), C = Cmax.

% Flood-fill outward through non-3 cells
outside(R2, C2) :-
    outside(R1, C1), delta(DR, DC),
    R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
    input(R2, C2, V), V != 3.

% Color-3 cells stay 3
:- input(R, C, 3), output(R, C, Color), Color != 3.

% Outside non-3 cells stay 0
:- outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 0.

% Interior non-3 cells (enclosed by 3s, not reachable from boundary) become 4
:- input(R, C, 0), not outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 4.
</asp>

% ------------------------------------------------------------------
% 1.  Grid dimensions
% ------------------------------------------------------------------
n_rows(N)   :- N = #count{R : input(R,_,_)}.
n_cols(M)   :- M = #count{C : input(_,C,_)}.

% ------------------------------------------------------------------
% 2.  4‑connected neighbourhood
% ------------------------------------------------------------------
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% ------------------------------------------------------------------
% 3.  Wall cells (colour 3)
% ------------------------------------------------------------------
non_wall(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), V != 3.

% ------------------------------------------------------------------
% 4.  Cells reachable from the boundary through non‑wall cells
% ------------------------------------------------------------------
boundary(R,C) :- R = 0.
boundary(R,C) :- R = N-1.
boundary(R,C) :- C = 0.
boundary(R,C) :- C = M-1.

outside_boundary(R,C) :- boundary(R,C), non_wall(R,C).

outside(R,C) :- outside_boundary(R,C).
outside(R,C) :-
    outside(R1,C1),
    delta(DR,DC),
    R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
    non_wall(R2,C2).

% ------------------------------------------------------------------
% 5.  Interior cells – non‑wall cells that cannot reach the boundary
% ------------------------------------------------------------------
interior(R,C) :- non_wall(R,C), not outside(R,C).

% ------------------------------------------------------------------
% 6.  Every cell must produce exactly one output colour
% ------------------------------------------------------------------
output_cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).               % true for every cell

% ------------------------------------------------------------------
% 7.  Colour constraints – they force a *single* colour for each cell
% ------------------------------------------------------------------
% 7.1  Interior cells become colour 2 (all other colours forbidden)
interior(R,C), output(R,C,Color), Color != 0.
interior(R,C), output(R,C,Color), Color != 1.
interior(R,C), output(R,C,Color), Color != 3.
interior(R,C), output(R,C,Color), Color != 4.
interior(R,C), output(R,C,Color), Color != 5.

interior(R,C), output(R,C,Color), Color != 9.

input(R,C,3), output(R,C,Color), Color != 1.
input(R,C,3), output(R,C,Color), Color != 2.
input(R,C,3), output(R,C,Color), Color != 4.

input(R,C,3), output(R,C,Color), Color != 9.

% 7.3  Cells with original colour 0 (outside) stay 0

input(R,C,0), not interior(R,C), output(R,C,Color), Color != 2.

<attempt_2>
<asp>
% -------------------------------------------------------------
% 1. Grid dimensions
% -------------------------------------------------------------
n_rows(N)   :- N = #count{R : input(R,_,_)}.
n_cols(M)   :- M = #count{C : input(_,C,_)}.

% -------------------------------------------------------------
% 2. 4‑connected neighbourhood
% -------------------------------------------------------------
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% -------------------------------------------------------------
% 3. non‑wall cells (value ≠ 3)
% -------------------------------------------------------------
non_wall(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), V != 3.

% -------------------------------------------------------------
% 4. Boundary cells (any cell on the outer edge)
% -------------------------------------------------------------
boundary(R,C) :- input(R,C,_), R = 0.
boundary(R,C) :- input(R,C,_), R = N-1.
boundary(R,C) :- input(R,C,_), C = 0.
boundary(R,C) :- input(R,C,_), C = M-1.

% -------------------------------------------------------------
% 5. Outside – reachable from the boundary through non‑wall cells
% -------------------------------------------------------------
outside(R,C) :- boundary(R,C), non_wall(R,C).
outside(R,C) :-
    outside(R1,C1),
    delta(DR,DC),
    R2 = R1+DR,
    C2 = C1+DC,
    input(R2,C2,V),
    V != 3.

% -------------------------------------------------------------
% 6. Interior – non‑wall cells that cannot reach the boundary
% -------------------------------------------------------------
interior(R,C) :- non_wall(R,C), not outside(R,C).

% -------------------------------------------------------------
% 7. Allowed colour for a cell (exactly one colour is allowed)
% -------------------------------------------------------------
allowed(R,C,Color) :- input(R,C,3),        Color = 3.                     % wall cells
allowed(R,C,Color) :- outside(R,C), input(R,C,0), Color = 0.           % outside zeros
allowed(R,C,Color) :- outside(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 0, Color = V.   % outside non‑zeros
allowed(R,C,Color) :- interior(R,C), input(R,C,0), Color = 2.           % interior zeros → colour 2
allowed(R,C,Color) :- interior(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 0, Color = V.   % interior non‑zeros keep their original colour

% -------------------------------------------------------------
% 8. Constraint – the chosen colour must be the allowed one
% -------------------------------------------------------------
:- not allowed(R,C,Color), output(R,C,Color).

% -------------------------------------------------------------
% 9. Exactly one output colour per cell
% -------------------------------------------------------------
output_cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).
</asp>

<asp>
% ----------  Grid dimensions ----------
n_rows(N)  :- N = #count{R : input(R,_,_)}.
n_cols(M)  :- M = #count{C : input(_,C,_)}.

% ----------  4‑connected neighbourhood ----------
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% ----------  neighbour relation (used for the flood‑fill ----------
neighbor(R1,C1,R2,C2) :-
    delta(DR,DC),
    R2 = R1+DR,
    C2 = C1+DC,
    input(R2,C2,_).

% ----------  source cells for the flood‑fill (border, non‑wall) ----------
%   border cells are those on the outer edge of the grid.
border(R,C) :- input(R,C,_), (R = 0 ; R = N-1 ; C = 0 ; C = M-1).

%   a border cell that is not a wall starts the outside region
outside(R,C) :- border(R,C), not input(R,C,3).

%   propagate the outside region through 4‑connected non‑wall cells
outside(R,C) :- outside(R1,C1), neighbor(R1,C1,R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 3.

% ----------  interior cells (non‑wall, not reachable from the border) ----------
interior(R,C) :- not input(R,C,3), not outside(R,C).

% ----------  each cell must have exactly one output colour ----------
output_cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

color(0..9).                         % injected automatically
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% ----------  colour constraints ----------
% 1. Wall cells (value 3) stay colour 3
:- input(R,C,3), output(R,C,Color), Color = 3.

% 2. Interior zero cells become colour 2
:- interior(R,C), input(R,C,0), output(R,C,Color), Color = 2.

% 3. Interior non‑zero (non‑wall) cells keep their original colour
:- interior(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 0, V != 3,
   output(R,C,Color), Color = V.

% 4. Outside zero cells stay zero
:- not interior(R,C), input(R,C,0), output(R,C,Color), Color = 0.

% 5. Outside non‑zero cells keep their original colour
:- not interior(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 0,
   output(R,C,Color), Color = V.
</asp>

<asp>
% 4‑connected neighbourhood offsets (kept for completeness)
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  Cells that contain a 2 – these will be the “seeds” that colour
%  interior regions.
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
seed2(R,C) :- input(R,C,2).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  Does a 2‑seed have a free neighbour to its left?
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
has_left(Rseed,Cseed) :-
    seed2(Rseed,Cseed),
    Cseed > 0,
    not input(Rseed,Cseed-1,3).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  Does a 2‑seed have a free neighbour below?
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
neighbor_down(Rseed,Cseed,Rd,Cd,V) :-
    delta(DR,DC),
    DR = 1, DC = 0,
    Rd = Rseed+DR,
    Cd = Cseed+DC,
    input(Rd,Cd,V).
has_down(Rseed,Cseed) :-
    seed2(Rseed,Cseed),
    neighbor_down(Rseed,Cseed,Rd,Cd,V),
    V != 3.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  Region coloured 2 (col2) – cells reachable from a 2‑seed
%  without crossing a 3‑wall, either leftward (if free left) or
%  downward (if free down).
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
col2(R,C) :-
    seed2(Rseed,Cseed),
    R = Rseed,
    C <= Cseed,
    has_left(Rseed,Cseed),
    not input(R,C,3).

col2(R,C) :-
    seed2(Rseed,Cseed),
    C = Cseed,
    R >= Rseed,
    has_down(Rseed,Cseed),
    not input(R,C,3).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  Exactly one colour is allowed for each cell.
%  - 3 stays 3 (wall)
%  - 2‑seeds colour their reachable interior cells 2
%  - 0’s outside the 2‑region stay 0
%  - 0’s inside the 2‑region become 2 (already covered by col2)
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
allowed(R,C,Color) :- input(R,C,3),        Color = 3.
allowed(R,C,Color) :- col2(R,C),        input(R,C,0), Color = 2.
allowed(R,C,Color) :- col2(R,C),        input(R,C,2), Color = 2.
allowed(R,C,Color) :- not col2(R,C),    input(R,C,0), Color = 0.
allowed(R,C,Color) :- not col2(R,C),    input(R,C,V), V != 0, Color = V.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
%  Each cell must produce exactly one output colour.
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
output_cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

% Choose the allowed colour for every output cell – exactly one
% answer set is forced.
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :-
    output_cell(R, C), allowed(R, C, Color).
</asp>

<asp>
% -------------------------------------------------------------
%  Grid dimensions
% -------------------------------------------------------------
n_rows(N)   :- N = #count{R : input(R,_,_)}.
n_cols(M)   :- M = #count{C : input(_,C,_)}.

% -------------------------------------------------------------
%  4‑connected neighbourhood (no extra delta predicate)
% -------------------------------------------------------------
neighbor(R1,C1,R,C) :-
    input(R1,C1,_),
    input(R,C,_),
    (R = R1+1, C = C1) ;      % up
neighbor(R1,C1,R,C) :-
    input(R1,C1,_),
    input(R,C,_),
    (R = R1-1, C = C1) ;      % down
neighbor(R1,C1,R,C) :-
    input(R1,C1,_),
    input(R,C,_),
    (R = R1,   C = C1+1) ;    % right
neighbor(R1,C1,R,C) :-
    input(R1,C1,_),
    input(R,C,_),
    (R = R1,   C = C1-1).     % left

% -------------------------------------------------------------
%  Border cells – any cell on the outer edge
% -------------------------------------------------------------
border(R,C) :- input(R,C,_), (R = 0 ; R = N-1 ; C = 0 ; C = M-1).

% -------------------------------------------------------------
%  Outside – start from border non‑wall cells and flood‑fill
% -------------------------------------------------------------
outside(R,C) :- border(R,C), not input(R,C,3).
outside(R,C) :-
    outside(R1,C1),
    neighbor(R1,C1,R,C),
    not input(R,C,3).

% -------------------------------------------------------------
%  Interior cells – non‑wall cells that cannot reach the border
% -------------------------------------------------------------
interior(R,C) :- not input(R,C,3), not outside(R,C).

% -------------------------------------------------------------
%  Colour that is allowed for each cell (exactly one colour)
% -------------------------------------------------------------
allowed(R,C,3)          :- input(R,C,3).                       % wall cells stay 3
allowed(R,C,Color)      :- interior(R,C), input(R,C,0),    Color = 2.   % interior zeros become 2
allowed(R,C,Color)      :- interior(R,C), input(R,C,V),    V != 0, V != 3,
                         Color = V.                                      % interior non‑zero keeps colour
allowed(R,C,Color)      :- not interior(R,C), input(R,C,0),   Color = 0.   % outside zeros stay 0
allowed(R,C,Color)      :- not interior(R,C), input(R,C,V),   V != 0,
                         Color = V.                                      % outside non‑zero keeps colour

% -------------------------------------------------------------
%  Every cell must produce exactly one output colour
% -------------------------------------------------------------
output_cell(R,C) :- true.                     % every cell of the output grid

1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :-
    output_cell(R, C), allowed(R, C, Color).
</asp>

<asp>
% Grid dimensions
n_rows(N)   :- N = #count{R : input(R,_,_)}.
n_cols(M)   :- M = #count{C : input(_,C,_)}.

% 4‑connected neighbourhood (constants only)
neighbor_up(R1,C1,R,C) :-
    input(R1,C1,_), input(R,C,_), R = R1-1, C = C1.
neighbor_down(R1,C1,R,C) :-
    input(R1,C1,_), input(R,C,_), R = R1+1, C = C1.
neighbor_left(R1,C1,R,C) :-
    input(R1,C1,_), input(R,C,_), R = R1, C = C1-1.
neighbor_right(R1,C1,R,C) :-
    input(R1,C1,_), input(R,C,_), R = R1, C = C1+1.

neighbor(R1,C1,R,C) :-
    neighbor_up(R1,C1,R,C);
neighbor(R1,C1,R,C) :-
    neighbor_down(R1,C1,R,C);
neighbor(R1,C1,R,C) :-
    neighbor_left(R1,C1,R,C);
neighbor(R1,C1,R,C) :-
    neighbor_right(R1,C1,R,C).

% Boundary cells (explicit)
border_left(R,C) :- input(R,C,_), R = 0.
border_right(R,C) :- input(R,C,_), R = #max{RR : input(RR,_,_)}.
border_top(R,C) :- input(R,C,_), C = 0.
border_bottom(R,C) :- input(R,C,_), C = #max{CC : input(_,CC,_)}.

% Starting outside: any border cell that is not a wall (colour 3)
outside(R,C) :- border_left(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 3.
outside(R,C) :- border_right(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 3.
outside(R,C) :- border_top(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 3.
outside(R,C) :- border_bottom(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 3.

% Propagate outside through non‑wall cells
outside(R,C) :- outside(R1,C1), neighbor(R1,C1,R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 3.

% Interior cells – non‑wall cells that cannot reach the boundary
interior(R,C) :- not input(R,C,3), not outside(R,C).

% Colour constraints
% Wall cells stay 3
allowed(R,C,3) :- input(R,C,3), Color = 3.
% Interior zeros become colour 2
allowed(R,C,Color) :- interior(R,C), input(R,C,0), Color = 2.
% Interior non‑zero (including 2) keep their colour
allowed(R,C,Color) :- interior(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 0, V != 3, Color = V.
% Outside zeros stay 0
allowed(R,C,0) :- not interior(R,C), input(R,C,0).
% Outside non‑zero keep their colour
allowed(R,C,Color) :- not interior(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 0, Color = V.

% Every cell must have exactly one output colour
output_cell(R,C) :- true.

% Exactly one colour per cell – the allowed colour must be chosen
:- not allowed(R,C,Color), output(R,C,Color).

1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :-
    output_cell(R, C), allowed(R, C, Color).
</asp>

% Count rows and columns
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R,_,_)}.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{C : input(_,C,_)}.

% Define max row/col for boundary
max_row(Rmax) :- Rmax = #max{RR : input(RR,_,_)}.
max_col(Cmax) :- Cmax = #max{CC : input(_,CC,_)}.

% Border predicates
border_left(R,C) :- input(R,C,_), R = 0.
border_right(R,C) :- input(R,C,_), R = max_row(Rmax).
border_top(R,C) :- input(R,C,_), C = 0.
border_bottom(R,C) :- input(R,C,_), C = max_col(Cmax).

% Outside starts from border cells that are not colour 3
outside(R,C) :- border_left(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 3.
outside(R,C) :- border_right(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 3.
outside(R,C) :- border_top(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 3.
outside(R,C) :- border_bottom(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 3.

% Flood fill: propagate through 4‑connected non‑wall cells
neighbor_up(R1,C1,R,C) :- input(R1,C1,_), input(R,C,_), R = R1-1, C = C1.
neighbor_down(R1,C1,R,C) :- input(R1,C1,_), input(R,C,_), R = R1+1, C = C1.
neighbor_left(R1,C1,R,C) :- input(R1,C1,_), input(R,C,_), R = R1, C = C1-1.
neighbor_right(R1,C1,R,C) :- input(R1,C1,_), input(R,C,_), R = R1, C = C1+1.

neighbor(R1,C1,R,C) :- neighbor_up(R1,C1,R,C).
neighbor(R1,C1,R,C) :- neighbor_down(R1,C1,R,C).
neighbor(R1,C1,R,C) :- neighbor_left(R1,C1,R,C).
neighbor(R1,C1,R,C) :- neighbor_right(R1,C1,R,C).

outside(R,C) :- outside(R1,C1), neighbor(R1,C1,R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 3.

% Interior cells
interior(R,C) :- not input(R,C,3), not outside(R,C).

% Allowed colours
% Wall cells
allowed(R,C,3) :- input(R,C,3), Color = 3.
% Interior zeros become 2
allowed(R,C,Color) :- interior(R,C), input(R,C,0), Color = 2.
% Interior non‑zero (including 2) keep original colour
allowed(R,C,Color) :- interior(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 0, V != 3, Color = V.
% Outside zeros stay 0
allowed(R,C,0) :- not interior(R,C), input(R,C,0).
% Outside non‑zero keep colour
allowed(R,C,Color) :- not interior(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 0, Color = V.

% Every cell must have an output colour (exactly one)
output_cell(R,C) :- true.

% Constraint: must choose allowed colour
:- not allowed(R,C,Color), output(R,C,Color).

% Exactly one answer set
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :-
    output_cell(R, C), allowed(R, C, Color).

% Grid dimensions
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R,_,_)}.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{C : input(_,C,_)}.

% Define max row/col
max_row(Rmax) :- Rmax = #max{RR : input(RR,_,_)}.
max_col(Cmax) :- Cmax = #max{CC : input(_,CC,_)}.

% Border predicates
border_left(R,C) :- input(R,C,_), R = 0.
border_right(R,C) :- input(R,C,_), R = max_row(Rmax).
border_top(R,C) :- input(R,C,_), C = 0.
border_bottom(R,C) :- input(R,C,_), C = max_col(Cmax).

% Outside: start from border cells that are not colour 3
outside(R,C) :- border_left(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 3.
outside(R,C) :- border_right(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 3.
outside(R,C) :- border_top(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 3.
outside(R,C) :- border_bottom(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 3.

% Flood fill through non‑wall cells
neighbor_up(R1,C1,R,C) :- input(R1,C1,_), input(R,C,_), R = R1-1, C = C1.
neighbor_down(R1,C1,R,C) :- input(R1,C1,_), input(R,C,_), R = R1+1, C = C1.
neighbor_left(R1,C1,R,C) :- input(R1,C1,_), input(R,C,_), R = R1, C = C1-1.
neighbor_right(R1,C1,R,C) :- input(R1,C1,_), input(R,C,_), R = R1, C = C1+1.
neighbor(R1,C1,R,C) :- neighbor_up(R1,C1,R,C).
neighbor(R1,C1,R,C) :- neighbor_down(R1,C1,R,C).
neighbor(R1,C1,R,C) :- neighbor_left(R1,C1,R,C).
neighbor(R1,C1,R,C) :- neighbor_right(R1,C1,R,C).

outside(R,C) :- outside(R1,C1), neighbor(R1,C1,R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 3.

% Interior cells (non‑wall cells not reachable from boundary)
interior(R,C) :- not input(R,C,3), not outside(R,C).

% Allowed colour for each cell
% Wall cells stay 3
allowed(R,C,3) :- input(R,C,3), Color = 3.
% Interior zeros become colour 2
allowed(R,C,Color) :- interior(R,C), input(R,C,0), Color = 2.
% Interior non‑zero cells keep their colour
allowed(R,C,Color) :- interior(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 0, V != 3, Color = V.
% Outside zeros stay 0
allowed(R,C,0) :- not interior(R,C), input(R,C,0).
% Outside non‑zero cells keep their colour
allowed(R,C,Color) :- not interior(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 0, Color = V.

% Every cell must have exactly one output colour
output_cell(R,C) :- true.

% Choose the allowed colour for each cell (exactly one answer set)
:- not allowed(R,C,Color), output(R,C,Color).

1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :-
    output_cell(R, C), allowed(R, C, Color).

<asp>
% Grid dimensions
n_rows(N)   :- N = #count{R : input(R,_,_)}.
n_cols(M)   :- M = #count{C : input(_,C,_)}.

% Maximum row and column indices (for the outer border)
max_row(Rmax) :- Rmax = #max{RR : input(RR,_,_)}.
max_col(Cmax) :- Cmax = #max{CC : input(_,CC,_)}.

% Border cells – explicit predicates for each side
border_left(R,C)  :- input(R,C,_), R = 0.
border_right(R,C) :- input(R,C,_), R = max_row(Rmax).
border_top(R,C)   :- input(R,C,_), C = 0.
border_bottom(R,C):- input(R,C,_), C = max_col(Cmax).

% Cells that start the “outside” region – any border cell that is not a colour‑3 wall
outside(R,C) :- border_left(R,C),   input(R,C,V), V != 3.
outside(R,C) :- border_right(R,C),  input(R,C,V), V != 3.
outside(R,C) :- border_top(R,C),    input(R,C,V), V != 3.
outside(R,C) :- border_bottom(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 3.

% 4‑connected neighbourhood (constant offsets – safe arithmetic)
neighbor_up(R1,C1,R,C)   :- input(R1,C1,_), input(R,C,_), R = R1-1, C = C1.
neighbor_down(R1,C1,R,C) :- input(R1,C1,_), input(R,C,_), R = R1+1, C = C1.
neighbor_left(R1,C1,R,C) :- input(R1,C1,_), input(R,C,_), R = R1,   C = C1-1.
neighbor_right(R1,C1,R,C) :- input(R1,C1,_), input(R,C,_), R = R1,   C = C1+1.
neighbor(R1,C1,R,C) :- neighbor_up(R1,C1,R,C).
neighbor(R1,C1,R,C) :- neighbor_down(R1,C1,R,C).
neighbor(R1,C1,R,C) :- neighbor_left(R1,C1,R,C).
neighbor(R1,C1,R,C) :- neighbor_right(R1,C1,R,C).

% Propagation of the “outside” region through non‑wall cells
outside(R,C) :- outside(R1,C1), neighbor(R1,C1,R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 3.

% Interior cells – non‑wall cells that cannot be reached from the boundary
interior(R,C) :- not input(R,C,3), not outside(R,C).

% Colour constraints – exactly one colour is allowed for each cell
% Wall cells (colour 3) stay colour 3
allowed(R,C,3) :- input(R,C,3), Color = 3.
% Interior zero cells become colour 2
allowed(R,C,Color) :- interior(R,C), input(R,C,0), Color = 2.
% Interior non‑zero cells (including colour 2) keep their original colour
allowed(R,C,Color) :- interior(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 0, V != 3, Color = V.
% Outside zero cells stay colour 0
allowed(R,C,Color) :- not interior(R,C), input(R,C,0), Color = 0.
% Outside non‑zero cells keep their colour
allowed(R,C,Color) :- not interior(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 0, Color = V.

% Every cell must have exactly one output colour
output_cell(R,C) :- true.

% The chosen colour must be the allowed colour for the cell
:- not allowed(R,C,Color), output(R,C,Color).

% Exactly one answer set – choose the allowed colour for every cell
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :-
    output_cell(R, C), allowed(R, C, Color).
</asp>

n_cols(M)   :- M = #count{C : input(_,C,_)}.

% Maximum row and column indices (for the outer border)
max_row(Rmax) :- Rmax = #max{RR : input(RR,_,_)}.
max_col(Cmax) :- Cmax = #max{CC : input(_,CC,_)}.

% Border cells – explicit predicates for each side
border_left(R,C)  :- input(R,C,_), R = 0.
border_right(R,C) :- input(R,C,_), R = max_row(Rmax).
border_top(R,C)   :- input(R,C,_), C = 0.
border_bottom(R,C):- input(R,C,_), C = max_col(Cmax).

% Cells that start the “outside” region – any border cell that is not a colour‑3 wall
outside(R,C) :- border_left(R,C),   input(R,C,V), V != 3.
outside(R,C) :- border_right(R,C),  input(R,C,V), V != 3.
outside(R,C) :- border_top(R,C),    input(R,C,V), V != 3.
outside(R,C) :- border_bottom(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 3.

% 4‑connected neighbourhood (constant offsets – safe arithmetic)
neighbor_up(R1,C1,R,C)   :- input(R1,C1,_), input(R,C,_), R = R1-1, C = C1.
neighbor_down(R1,C1,R,C) :- input(R1,C1,_), input(R,C,_), R = R1+1, C = C1.
neighbor_left(R1,C1,R,C) :- input(R1,C1,_), input(R,C,_), R = R1,   C = C1-1.
neighbor_right(R1,C1,R,C) :- input(R1,C1,_), input(R,C,_), R = R1,   C = C1+1.
neighbor(R1,C1,R,C) :- neighbor_up(R1,C1,R,C).
neighbor(R1,C1,R,C) :- neighbor_down(R1,C1,R,C).
neighbor(R1,C1,R,C) :- neighbor_left(R1,C1,R,C).
neighbor(R1,C1,R,C) :- neighbor_right(R1,C1,R,C).

% Propagation of the “outside” region through non‑wall cells
outside(R,C) :- outside(R1,C1), neighbor(R1,C1,R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 3.

% Interior cells – non‑wall cells that cannot be reached from the boundary
interior(R,C) :- not input(R,C,3), not outside(R,C).

% Colour constraints – exactly one colour is allowed for each cell
% Wall cells (colour 3) stay colour 3
allowed(R,C,3) :- input(R,C,3), Color = 3.
% Interior zero cells become colour 2
allowed(R,C,Color) :- interior(R,C), input(R,C,0), Color = 2.
% Interior non‑zero cells (including colour 2) keep their original colour
allowed(R,C,Color) :- interior(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 0, V != 3, Color = V.
% Outside zero cells stay colour 0
allowed(R,C,Color) :- not interior(R,C), input(R,C,0), Color = 0.
% Outside non‑zero cells keep their colour
allowed(R,C,Color) :- not interior(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 0, Color = V.

% Every cell must have exactly one output colour
output_cell(R,C) :- true.

% The chosen colour must be the allowed colour for the cell
:- not allowed(R,C,Color), output(R,C,Color).

% Exactly one answer set – choose the allowed colour for every cell
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :-
